title: Cnidoscolus acrandrus
intro: Cnidoscolus acrandrus, also known as chiltepín or chile tepin, is a species of cactus-like plant in the family Euphorbiaceae, native to Mexico and Central America. The health benefits of Cnidoscolus acrandrus include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antibacterial and antifungal properties, making it a potential treatment for various health conditions. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Cnidoscolus acrandrus is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture in traditional medicine, and is also used in modern herbalism to promote cardiovascular health and immune function.

This family includes over 2,700 species, many of which are native to tropical and subtropical regions of the world. side_effects: Cnidoscolus acrandrus, commonly known as the cowhage or cow itch, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. However, it may cause skin irritation, allergic reactions, and itching due to the presence of urticating hairs. These hairs can cause intense itching and skin irritation, often leading to a condition known as cow itch. It is essential to exercise caution when handling or consuming this plant due to its potential side effects.
